growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: 0.6 M Na+ soda buffer mineral medium with thiocyanate

up.method: calculated: volumetric consumption = D x feed conc = 0.043 h-1 x 10 mM = 0.43 mmol/L/h, assuming ~complete consumption of the growth-limiting substrate at steady state

sec.method: calculated: paper states 10 mM cyanate is formed from thiocyanate oxidation; at steady state production = D x 10 mM = 0.043 x 10 = 0.43 mmol/L/h

growth_records.cite: Berben et al. Analysis of the Genes Involved in Thiocyanate Oxidation during Growth in Continuous Culture of the Haloalkaliphilic Sulfur-Oxidizing Bacterium Thioalkalivibrio thiocyanoxidans ARh 2T Using Transcriptomics. mSystems (2017). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00102-17. [PMC5744179]
growth_records.snippet: At steady state, with an optical density at 600 nm (OD600) of 0.13 +/- 0.01 for thiocyanate cultures... The dilution rate was set at 0.043 h-1. Either potassium thiocyanate at 10 mM ... was used as an electron donor and sulfur source.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state so mu = D = 0.043 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.043 = 16.12 h. Only OD600 (0.13) reported, no gDW biomass, so specific rates (mmol/gDW/h) are NOT derivable; volumetric uptake/secretion computed as D x feed concentration assuming complete consumption of the limiting substrate at steady state: thiocyanate 0.043 x 10 mM = 0.43 mmol/L/h; cyanate produced 0.043 x 10 mM = 0.43 mmol/L/h (10 mM cyanate stated to form). Elemental sulfur is also produced but no rate given. Temperature 30C, 300 rpm stirring, pH 9.8, air 2 L/min (aerobic; thiocyanate oxidation stated to be strictly aerobic).

growth_records.medium: 0.6 M Na+ soda buffer mineral medium with thiosulfate

up.method: calculated: volumetric consumption = D x feed conc = 0.043 h-1 x 40 mM = 1.72 mmol/L/h, assuming ~complete consumption of the growth-limiting substrate at steady state
calculated: D x feed conc = 0.043 x 5 mM = 0.215 mmol/L/h (N source)

growth_records.cite: Berben et al. Analysis of the Genes Involved in Thiocyanate Oxidation during Growth in Continuous Culture of the Haloalkaliphilic Sulfur-Oxidizing Bacterium Thioalkalivibrio thiocyanoxidans ARh 2T Using Transcriptomics. mSystems (2017). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00102-17. [PMC5744179]
growth_records.snippet: OD600 of ... 0.34 +/- 0.01 for thiosulfate cultures... sodium thiosulfate (Na2S2O3 . 5H2O) at 40 mM ... was used as an electron donor and sulfur source. For growth with thiosulfate, ammonium chloride (NH4Cl) at 5 mM was added as the nitroge
growth_records.notes: Same chemostat run as the thiocyanate condition but with thiosulfate as electron donor. Chemostat steady state so mu = D = 0.043 h-1; td = ln2/0.043 = 16.12 h. Only OD600 (0.34) reported, no gDW biomass, so specific rates (mmol/gDW/h) NOT derivable; volumetric uptake computed as D x feed concentration assuming complete consumption at steady state: thiosulfate 0.043 x 40 mM = 1.72 mmol/L/h; ammonium 0.043 x 5 mM = 0.215 mmol/L/h. Elemental sulfur produced but no rate given. 30C, 300 rpm, pH 9.8, air 2 L/min.
